The problem we solve

You have a Python bot.
Now you need to keep it running.

Today, anyone can ask an AI to write a trading bot in Python in just a few minutes — RSI, MACD, scalping, grid trading… the code exists, the strategies too. But once you have the .py file, the real challenge begins: how do you keep it running non-stop, without leaving your PC on, without losing it if it crashes?

Binance Bot Executor is the answer. It's a web interface that handles the execution of your Python bots on Binance: drop your script, configure your API keys, and the tool takes care of the rest — supervision, automatic restarts, alerts, live logs.

🤖

AI-generated bots

Get your strategy written by ChatGPT or Claude, drop the .py file, and deploy in one click.

⏰

24/7 execution

Your bots run continuously on the server, even when your computer is off.

🔄

Auto-restart

If a bot crashes or stops unexpectedly, it restarts automatically — no action needed.

🔔

Telegram & Discord alerts

Get an instant notification whenever a bot starts, stops, or crashes.

🔐

Secure API keys

Your Binance keys are AES-128 encrypted in the database. They never appear in plain text.

🧪

Testnet mode

Test your strategy on the Binance testnet with zero risk before going live with real capital.

Who is it for?

Built for anyone who wants
to automate their trading

No need to be a developer. If you have a Python script — no matter how you got it — this tool is made for you.

🧑‍💼

The curious trader

You asked an AI for an RSI bot and want to run it for real.

👩‍💻

The Python developer

You write your own strategies and need a simple way to deploy them.

You want to automate a repetitive strategy without watching the markets all day.

You're exploring algo trading and want to test your ideas on the Binance testnet.

Secure access

A password-protected
interface

Access is secured by a personal password. Even if the tool runs on a remote server, only you control your bots.

The interface is available in English and French, with a language selector right on the login page.

✅

Your password can be updated at any time from the Settings page.

Overview

The Dashboard: everything
at a glance

As soon as you log in, the dashboard gives you an instant snapshot of your entire trading infrastructure.

Total Bots
Configured bots
Active Bots
Currently running
Errors
Bots with errors

Live activity feed

The bottom of the dashboard shows a live log stream: every decision your bot makes is recorded with a timestamp, Binance price, indicator values (RSI…), and the signal emitted — NEUTRAL, BUY, or SELL. Perfect for understanding and debugging your strategy at any time.

Bot management

My Bots: all your scripts
in one place

The My Bots page lists your bots as visual cards — name, description, linked Python file, and live status, all at a glance.

A single ▶ Play button launches any bot. Sort by name, date, or status as your list grows.

Quick search Filter by status Multi-select Card view
Getting started

Deploy your bot
in 4 simple steps

1

Name your bot

Give it a clear label, e.g. "BTC/USDT RSI Scalping".

2

Upload your .py script

Drag and drop your Python file — AI-generated or hand-coded. 5 MB max.

3

Link a Binance API key

It's injected as a secure environment variable — never exposed in code.

4

Configure via JSON

Pass your parameters (symbol, interval, thresholds…) without touching the code.

🔄

Enable auto-restart to automatically recover from unexpected crashes.

Security

Your Binance API keys,
always protected

Store your Binance API keys (Mainnet or Testnet) directly in the tool. They are AES-128 encrypted in the database and never exposed in plain text.

Each key can be linked to one or more bots. The interface shows the date added, network type, and how many bots are using it.

⚠️

Only grant your API key the permissions it actually needs. For testing, use testnet.binance.vision — zero risk to your real capital.

Notifications

Stay informed
at every moment

The Settings page brings everything together: password, language, and most importantly real-time alerts on your bots.

📱

Telegram

Enter your Bot Token + Chat ID — receive alerts directly as private messages.

💬

Discord

Add a Webhook URL to receive alerts in your Discord server.

🔔

Choose when to be notified: crash, stop, start — or all three.
